features: F16: Real-Time Streaming in the UI — useStreamingResponse hook reads SSE stream token-by-token, typing indicator shown before first token, smooth transition to persisted state on stream end
F17: Optimistic Message Updates — User message appears instantly on send with temporary ID, removed with error toast if API call fails
F18: Source Citations Display — Collapsible Sources section beneath assistant messages showing cited video titles as styled chips/tags
F19: Video Knowledge Base Explorer — Slide-over panel or modal accessible from sidebar showing all ingested videos as cards with title, description, and link
F20: Empty States & Error Handling — Empty conversation prompt with starter questions, empty sidebar CTA, inline API error with retry, auto-dismissing network error toast

criteria.name: sse_stream_renders_incrementally
criteria.description: When a user sends a message, the assistant bubble begins populating with text before the full response is complete. Observing the DOM at 500ms intervals during a response, at least 3 distinct intermediate text states must be captured before the final content appears — confirming token-by-token rendering via the SSE ReadableStream.

criteria.name: typing_indicator_shown_before_first_token
criteria.description: Immediately after a message is submitted and before the first SSE token arrives, a typing indicator (three pulsing dots) must appear inside a left-aligned assistant-style bubble. The indicator must be visible for the full duration of any latency before the first token and must disappear — without a flash or layout shift — once the first streaming token replaces it. If the first token arrives in under 100ms, the indicator is still expected to render at least one frame before being replaced.

criteria.name: streaming_disables_chat_input
criteria.description: While a response is actively streaming, the chat textarea and send button must both be visually disabled (greyed out) and non-interactive. Attempts to submit another message during streaming must be ignored. Input re-enables within 300ms of the stream completing.

criteria.name: optimistic_message_appears_instantly
criteria.description: The user's message must appear in the chat message list within 100ms of pressing Send or hitting Enter — before any API response is received. The message must display the correct content and be right-aligned in the user bubble style, with a temporary ID that does not persist as undefined or null in the rendered DOM.

criteria.name: optimistic_message_removed_on_api_failure
criteria.description: If the POST to /api/conversations/{id}/messages returns a non-2xx status or a network error occurs, the optimistically inserted user message must be removed from the message list within 500ms of the failure. A visible error toast must appear in the top-right corner. Additionally, the user's original message text must be restored to the chat input field so they can retry or edit without retyping. The chat input must be re-enabled immediately upon failure.

criteria.name: error_toast_auto_dismisses
criteria.description: Network and API error toast notifications must automatically dismiss between 3.5 and 4.5 seconds after appearing without any user interaction. The toast must also be manually dismissible via a visible close (×) button before the auto-dismiss timer expires. The toast must be visually distinct (positioned top-right, using the danger color #ef4444), and must not block interaction with the rest of the UI while visible.

criteria.name: source_citations_rendered_when_sources_present
criteria.description: Assistant messages that include RAG-retrieved source citations must display a collapsible 'Sources' section below the message content. The section must show each cited video as a chip/tag styled with a blue border and the video title text. The section must be collapsed by default and expand/collapse on click without a page reload or loss of message content. Assistant messages for which no source citations were returned must NOT render a Sources section at all — no empty collapsed section or 'No sources' placeholder should appear.

criteria.name: source_citations_data_accuracy
criteria.description: The video titles shown in the Sources section must correspond to actual videos stored in the database that were retrieved during RAG — not hardcoded or fabricated values. Each chip must display a recognizable title string matching entries returned by GET /api/videos. The number of chips must match the number of distinct sources returned for that message. If a message response contains zero citations, no Sources section is rendered (see source_citations_rendered_when_sources_present).

criteria.name: video_explorer_panel_opens_and_displays_all_videos
criteria.description: Clicking the Library/Sources icon button in the sidebar must open a slide-over panel or modal. While videos are loading, a visible loading indicator (spinner or skeleton cards) must be shown. Once loaded, the panel must list every video returned by GET /api/videos as individual cards, each showing the video title, a short description, and a URL link. The count of rendered cards must exactly match the count returned by the API. The panel must be dismissible by clicking outside it or a close button. If the API call fails, an inline error message must appear inside the panel rather than a blank or broken layout.

criteria.name: empty_conversation_state_shown_with_starters
criteria.description: When navigating to a new conversation that has no messages, the chat area must display a centered empty state containing: an icon or illustration, the text 'Ask anything about the video library' (or equivalent), and at least 2 clickable suggested starter question prompts. Clicking a starter question must populate the chat input with that question text without auto-submitting — the user must still press Send to submit.

criteria.name: empty_sidebar_state_shown_with_cta
criteria.description: If no conversations exist in the database, the sidebar conversation list must display an empty state message (e.g. 'No conversations yet') along with a visible call-to-action (button or link) that navigates the user to start a new chat. The empty state must not display a blank white box or an unhandled undefined error. A loading spinner or skeleton must appear during the initial fetch so the empty state is not prematurely shown before data has loaded.

criteria.name: inline_api_error_with_retry_button
criteria.description: If the streaming message endpoint fails after the user submits a message (including mid-stream SSE failures after partial content has been rendered), an inline error message must appear within the chat area — below the last message or in place of the failed assistant bubble — with a 'Retry' button. Clicking Retry must re-attempt the same API call with the same message content without requiring the user to retype it. Both the inline error and the toast notification must appear simultaneously on failure; they are not mutually exclusive.

criteria.name: mid_stream_failure_handled_gracefully
criteria.description: If the SSE connection drops or returns an error event after streaming has already begun (i.e., partial assistant content is visible), the partial content must be removed from the chat and replaced with an inline error state containing a Retry button. No partial or truncated assistant message must remain persisted in the UI. The chat input must be re-enabled and the user's original message must be restored to the input field.

criteria.name: auto_scroll_behavior_correct
criteria.description: When new tokens stream in, the chat area must auto-scroll to the bottom to keep the latest content visible — unless the user has manually scrolled up more than 100px from the bottom, in which case auto-scroll must be paused. Auto-scroll must resume automatically when the user scrolls back to within 100px of the bottom. This behavior must work for both streaming tokens and newly loaded historical messages.

criteria.name: streaming_to_persisted_state_transition
criteria.description: When the SSE stream ends, the transient streaming content must seamlessly transition to the persisted message state. There must be no visible flash, content duplication, or message disappearing during this transition. The final persisted message content must exactly match the full streamed content.

criteria.name: design_consistency_sprint5_components
criteria.description: All new Sprint 5 UI components (source chips, video explorer cards, toast notifications, empty states, typing indicator) must use the defined color palette: backgrounds from #0a0a0f/#111827/#1e293b, accent blue #3b82f6 for interactive elements, danger #ef4444 for error states, and text in #f1f5f9/#94a3b8. Verification method: grep component source files for hardcoded hex values or inline style attributes that fall outside this palette — zero violations permitted. Tailwind utility classes mapping to these values are acceptable.
